summary: Phase-2 SILENT-FALLBACK fix (implausible F1>900 Hz after all LPC-ceiling retries 5500→5000→4500 → HTTP 422 + WARNING logs, never a silent misleading score) verified end-to-end via /api/phonemes/analyze-formants. Extended /app/backend/tests/test_phoneme_formants_phase2.py from 29 → 35 tests (all passing). Added 5 new test classes covering every review-request requirement: (1) TestSilentFallbackImplausibleReturns422 — synthetic vowel with F1~1250 (F2~1700, F3~2600, F0=120, ~400ms + 100ms leading silence, 44.1kHz mono 16-bit) posted with phoneme_ipa='ʊ' returns HTTP 422 with detail containing 'non affidabile', and neither composite_score nor student_formants leak in the body; (2) TestSilentFallbackWarningLogs — after the implausible request the backend log contains a WARNING 'analyze-formants: implausible F1=<val> Hz after all LPC-ceiling retries' AND a WARNING 'REJECTED unreliable measurement F1=<val>' with F1 parsed from BOTH lines confirmed >900; (3) TestNormalUReturnsReliable200 — a normal /ʊ/-like vowel (F1≈430, F2≈1100, F3≈2400) returns 200 with student_formants.reliable==True, F1<=550, composite_score present, cefr.band present; (4) TestSilentFallbackReproducibility5x — KEY ACCEPTANCE TEST: 5x identical POST of the normal /ʊ/ WAV → all 200 with reliable=True and IDENTICAL F1/F0/reference_group/composite_score across all 5 runs; 5x identical POST of the implausible WAV → all 5 consistently 422 (never a silent 200 smuggling a score); (5) TestSilentFallbackRetriesCeilings — verifies via inspect.getsource that _extract_formants iterates the three ceilings 5500/5000/4500 and sets reliable=False on the fallback. Regression coverage untouched: F0 thresholds (120→men, 200→women, 280→children), consent gate 403→200, dataset ʊ AmE word target, /iː/ RP low F1, diphthong /aɪ/ absent-from-dataset teacher_sample fallback, _weighted_composite([0,59,88])=62.5≥60 all continue to pass.
critical_code_review_comments: phoneme_formants.py:_extract_formants — the fallback path currently returns the FIRST implausible measurement encountered across the 3 ceiling retries (5500 first). If a later ceiling (e.g. 4500) happens to produce a slightly less-implausible F1 (still >900 but closer to plausible), the logged/reported F1 is arbitrary. Consider returning the MIN-F1 fallback across the 3 retries so the log & payload are more informative for debugging noisy student mics.

F1_MAX=900 is a hard-coded module-level constant. It is correct for adult vowels but children can legitimately reach F1~1000+ for open front vowels like /æ/. Given the router already knows whether the F0-selected group is 'children' (F0>255), consider lifting F1_MAX to a per-group ceiling (e.g. F1_MAX_ADULT=900, F1_MAX_CHILD=1200) so child recordings do not get 422'd on legitimately high F1. This is an enhancement, not a regression.

The 422 response detail is Italian only ('Misura non affidabile...'). Given the app targets English pronunciation learners with an Italian teacher, this is acceptable, but the API contract mixes languages (validation errors are localised, other errors are not). Consider a locale-aware error middleware or standardised i18n codes when the frontend needs to surface these consistently.

The two WARNING log lines are emitted from two different code paths (one from _extract_formants, one from analyze_formants after the reliable=False check). If the log volume ever becomes a concern, they could be consolidated into a single structured WARNING with the same information — but the current split is intentional and makes RCA easier.

TestSilentFallbackReproducibility5x is the strongest guarantee of the fix (5x identical bytes → 5x identical scored payload OR 5x consistent 422). Consider running it as a smoke test on every deploy — it is fast (~10 s) and catches any regression to non-deterministic behaviour immediately.
seed_data_creation: None — reused existing formant_references seeds (AmE men/women/children; RP male/female) and the pre-seeded teacher u-foot mp3 sample.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_phoneme_formants_phase2.py now has 35 tests (was 29). New SILENT-fallback bug-fix classes appended at end of file: TestSilentFallbackImplausibleReturns422 (1 test — implausible /ʊ/ → 422 + Italian detail + no composite_score/student_formants leaks); TestSilentFallbackWarningLogs (1 test — both 'implausible F1=<val>' and 'REJECTED unreliable measurement F1=<val>' WARNING lines with F1>900); TestNormalUReturnsReliable200 (1 test — normal /ʊ/ → 200 reliable=True composite_score+cefr present); TestSilentFallbackReproducibility5x (2 tests — 5x normal identical / 5x implausible all 422); TestSilentFallbackRetriesCeilings (1 test — code inspection: 5500/5000/4500 ceilings + reliable=False). New module-scoped fixtures: implausible_wav (F1~1250) and normal_u_wav (F1~430). To run: cd /app/backend && REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://canonical-voice-lab.preview.emergentagent.com python -m pytest tests/test_phoneme_formants_phase2.py -v
rca of the issue: Root cause (as fixed by main agent in /app/backend/routers/phoneme_formants.py before this test iteration): the min-variance nucleus picker in _formants_for_ceiling returns the least-SD 20ms window. When NO window's median F1 passed the F1<=900 Hz plausibility check, the OLD code silently fell back to the least-variance window regardless of its implausible F1 and returned it — the analyze_formants endpoint then scored it as if valid, producing wildly different composites (e.g. same /iː/ speaker: 343 Hz → 73/100 B1 one session vs 933 Hz → 16/100 A1 the next). Fix (verified in this iteration): (a) _extract_formants now RETRIES with alternative LPC ceilings 5500→5000→4500 Hz and returns the FIRST plausible result with reliable=True; (b) if every retry still yields median F1>900 Hz, it returns the fallback with reliable=False and emits WARNING 'analyze-formants: implausible F1=<val> Hz after all LPC-ceiling retries'; (c) analyze_formants now raises HTTP 422 with detail 'Misura non affidabile...' when student.get('reliable') is False, plus a second WARNING 'REJECTED unreliable measurement F1=<val>'. Reproducibility: the 5x-identical-POST acceptance test proves the fix — same bytes now give either 5x identical scored 200 OR 5x consistent 422, never the silent flip between the two.
